forecaster_note: Exposures clean up as the SE swell eases and winds go W — clean head to overhead surf at exposed breaks this morning, with smaller but still fun options elsewhere. Slightly smaller into the afternoon but winds stay light and variable to offshore with clean conditions all day. Sat: SE energy fades, still fun-sized surf and clean options all day. Sun: small but worth a paddle with all-day offshores — crisp and clean. INCOMING: the next SE to E swell fills in from Wednesday for another round of decent to maybe solid size. — Hugh McDowell (Surfline)

wind.summary: WSW 8kph (16 gusts) — light offshore this morning, staying light and variable-to-offshore all day. Clean conditions everywhere, no arvo blowout.

shark.summary: No shark detections on the Northern Beaches (Palm Beach → Manly) in the past 24h — drumline, drone, SLS and trauma feeds all quiet. Only notices: SLS drones not flying at Palm Beach, Warriewood and Collaroy yesterday. Usual drill — scan the lineup, surf with company.

days.label: Today
days.note: Best day of the week — the SE swell is easing but there's still plenty of it, and the wind is finally straight offshore WSW all day. Exposed breaks head to overhead early, cleaner everywhere. Low tide 9:55am puts your 10–12 window right on the incoming push.

dee-why.window: Morning is the call — easing SE 128° swell (a good angle here, no dead-S wrap) with straight offshore WSW. The 10–12 slot rides the push off the 9:55am low; slightly smaller by the arvo but clean all day.

tip.label: Green light
tip.text: 373kJ at 92/100 consistency with genuine offshore — clean head-high-plus faces. Frontside rights at the Point on the incoming after the 9:55am low; performance board conditions.

long-reef.window: Biggest of the four — 502kJ and 100/100 consistency, but the WSW blows cross-shore over the reefs. Outer bombies 2x-OH for the committed; go early before any afternoon shift.

tip.label: Step-up territory
tip.text: Surfline's call: ride a step-up in 4–5ft. Max juice, less shape — only if you want size today.

curl-curl.window: The S-magnet is overhead-plus with WSW cross-shore — the south corner is tidiest. Mid-morning on the push after the 9:55am low.

tip.label: South corner
tip.text: 389kJ / 94 consistency — plenty of wave. Backside lefts on the bank or the righthander off the southern rocks frontside.

manly.window: Cleanest-looking option — straight offshore and fair-to-good, but consistency is only 59/100 so expect waits between sets. Fun all-rounder waves through the morning.

tip.label: Patience pays
tip.text: Half the consistency of Dee Why (59 vs 92) — bring the all-rounder and pick off the sets rather than grinding for scraps.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: SE energy fades but it's still fun-sized, with clean options all day on light W–offshore flow. The classic weekend-warrior Saturday: no wind pressure, so the mid-morning 10–12 slot works fine (low ~10:53am).

dee-why.window: Fun-size leftovers, clean all day — mid-morning on the incoming after the ~10:53am low.
alerts: ⚠️ Dee Why day-2 table didn’t load on Surfline this run — size interpolated from Long Reef/Curl Curl

long-reef.window: Holds the most leftover SE energy — reefs still overhead on sets early.

curl-curl.window: Fun beachie banks, clean all day — south end for a touch more size.

manly.window: Smaller and inconsistent but groomed — good for a cruise if the crowd at DY annoys you.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: Sat: fading but fun-sized SE leftovers, clean all day — the better surf day. Sun: small (1–2ft) but crisp with all-day offshores — fish/groveler territory or a longboard cruise. Next solid SE–E swell fills in from Wednesday.

dee-why.window: Sat morning is the wave-count session; Sun is small, crisp and uncrowded — bring volume.
alerts: ⚠️ Dee Why extended table didn’t load on Surfline this run — size interpolated from neighbouring spots

long-reef.window: Best size-holder on Sunday — reefs pick up what little SE energy remains.

curl-curl.window: Small clean beachie peaks Sunday — fun on a groveler at the banks.

manly.window: Tiny but tidy — log or fish only on Sunday.
